fields.excerpt: נְטֵה לְךָ עַל יְמִינְךָ אוֹ עַל שְׂמֹאלֶךָ
fields.summary: The child quotes this verse as a prooftext, implying that the path is clear and safe, similar to the servant's request for guidance.

fields.excerpt: עָשִׂיתִי לִי שָׁרִים וְשָׁרוֹת וְתַעֲנוּגוֹת בְּנֵי הָאָדָם, שִׁדָּה וְשִׁדּוֹת
fields.summary: The verse is cited and then interpreted phrase by phrase: 'sharim v'sharot' as musical instruments, 'ta'anugot bnei ha'adam' as baths and pools, and 'shidah v'shidot' as female and male demons.

fields.excerpt: וְהַבַּיִת בְּהִבָּנוֹתוֹ אֶבֶן שְׁלֵמָה מַסָּע נִבְנָה
fields.summary: The verse is cited to explain the need for the shamir worm, as the Temple was built without iron tools, requiring a miraculous substance to cut the stones.

fields.excerpt: לֵץ הַיַּיִן הוֹמֶה שֵׁכָר, וְכׇל שׁוֹגֶה בּוֹ לֹא יֶחְכָּם
fields.summary: Ashmedai quotes this verse to justify his refusal to drink the wine, as it warns against the folly of drunkenness.
Ashmedai also cites this verse to reinforce the danger of wine, which leads to moral corruption and loss of understanding.
